summary: Grow the Skill and Agent typed field schemas to the current documented frontmatter — the optional keys the skills/sub-agents pages document (fields only, no new clauses)

edit.description: Add the current documented optional frontmatter fields as typed properties. On the `Skill` interface (kebab-case keys): `when_to_use`, `argument-hint`, `arguments`, `allowed-tools`, `disallowed-tools`, `model`, `effort`, `context`, `agent`, `hooks`, `shell` — the ones not already present (disable-model-invocation/user-invocable/paths/license already ship). On the `Agent` interface (camelCase keys): `tools`, `disallowedTools`, `model`, `permissionMode`, `maxTurns`, `skills`, `mcpServers`, `hooks`, `memory`, `background`, `effort`, `isolation`, `color`, `initialPrompt`. Each optional, each with a per-field doc-comment `cite` re-fetched raw against code.claude.com/docs/en/{skills,sub-agents}. `command` inherits Skill's schema by import. No clauses — fields only.

tests.asserts: A skill and an agent authored with a sample of the new fields project them to YAML frontmatter in declared order, byte-faithfully (the generic fold already carries them; the assertion pins the typed surface).

acceptance: An author can type every documented Skill/Agent frontmatter field with hover guidance and a cite, and a member carrying them projects them byte-faithfully to frontmatter; `pnpm --dir sdk test` and `cargo test` (builtin_lock_frozen) stay green (builtins.md: the documented fields that modulate them per member are ordinary declared fields).
notes: Verified on disk at abd9a58 (window 4c428b5..HEAD is flume-only): the reported Skill keys are absent, Agent has only description/prose. Field folding is generic (kind.ts Object.entries minus FRAMEWORK_KEYS) + generic frontmatter adapter → SDK-typing-only, no Rust/lock ripple (builtin members set none). Skill keys kebab-case, agent camelCase; build re-fetches raw per field per cite (market-formats.md deep-audit 2/6). Clauses excluded — (builtins-coverage-predicates) fork. scoped at abd9a58.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead — release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. scoped at b676656.
